# Public Data Collection Checklist

Use only public or authorized data. Respect platform terms, privacy law, and academic ethics.

## Before collection

- Define the research question.
- Define the signal being tested.
- Confirm the data source is public or authorized.
- Record platform terms and limitations.
- Avoid private messages, doxxing, or sensitive personal data.
- Decide what you will not collect.

## During collection

Record:

- timestamp
- source URL
- object ID
- actor ID
- action type
- content text/caption if lawful
- disclosure status
- engagement metrics
- screenshots only when necessary
- archive timestamp

## After collection

- De-identify where possible.
- Store raw and processed data separately.
- Preserve a chain of evidence.
- Keep method notes.
- Record limitations.

## Do not

- scrape prohibited data
- bypass rate limits or access controls
- collect private personal information
- publish unverified accusations
